summary: Crisp sound‑board capture of a classic spring‑1990 show highlighted by an epic Scarlet > Fire and tight first‑set jams.
review: The recording is a solid SBD that most listeners describe as clear and well balanced, with only a handful of complaints about vocal levels or occasional drop‑outs. Musically the concert shines – the 30‑minute Scarlet > Fire sequence, a scorching Ramble On Rose, and an enthusiastic Baby Blue encore are repeatedly called out as standouts, while even the humorous missteps in Hell In A Bucket add character rather than detract. Overall the performance is regarded as one of the stronger shows from the 1990 spring tour.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, the vast majority give 4‑5 stars and praise both the music (especially Scarlet > Fire) and the soundboard quality; a few isolated criticisms lower the average slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers note crisp, clear SBD audio with good instrument separation. A single reviewer calls it "lousy" and another mentions vocals a bit high, but overall the mix is praised as well‑balanced.

poor_songs: Hell In A Bucket (vocal flubs mentioned by some)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight, joke‑filled first set leads into an expansive, relaxed second set where the long Scarlet > Fire jam serves as the centerpiece; transitions and banter keep the energy high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for tasteful guitar tone and creative midi textures during Scarlet > Fire; occasional vocal flubs noted but not a major issue.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as "sick" on bass, driving the jams and delivering standout moments (e.g., Lovelight intro, Phil‑centric improvisations).
band_member_comments.Bob: His banter (“Are you ready for Star Time?”) and humorous lyric slips in Hell In A Bucket are highlighted as entertaining highlights.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboard work is called solid and well‑in‑time; some note the midi effects add color to Scarlet > Fire.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy & Mickey receive praise for tight, grooving playing that underpins the extended jams.
